title: The No-Nonsense Guide to Digital Product Payment Solutions in West Africa — The Full Guide
author: Unchained Commerce
excerpt: Selling digital products from Nigeria, Ghana, Kenya, Pakistan, or Bangladesh is possible with crypto-native infrastructure that has no geographic restrictions.

content.text: If you're a digital creator in West Africa, you're likely familiar with the frustration of trying to sell your ebooks, courses, or art internationally. Platforms like PayPal and Stripe often block transactions from countries like Nigeria, Ghana, Kenya, and South Africa due to 'geo-restrictions.' Even if you manage to get past these hurdles, you're still left with the challenge of finding a reliable payment method that works across borders. In this guide, we'll show you how to break free from these limitations and start selling your digital products globally without any hassle.
Why This Method Works When Everything Else Fails

The problem with traditional payment solutions like PayPal and Stripe is that they rely on your bank account and personal identity to process transactions. But what if you're a digital creator in a country with limited banking options or strict KYC (Know Your Customer) regulations? That's where blockchain-based payments come in – they allow you to receive payments directly from your buyers, without the need for a bank account or personal identification. By using a payment processor like NOWPayments, you can accept over 300 different cryptocurrencies and break free from the limitations of traditional payment solutions.
What You Need Before You Start

Before you can start selling your digital products, you'll need to set up a payment and delivery pipeline. This involves creating a unique URL for each product, integrating a payment processor like NOWPayments, and setting up automated delivery for your buyers. You'll also need to decide on a fair pricing strategy and create a marketing plan to reach your target audience. Don't worry, we'll walk you through each step of the process in this guide.
Setting Up Your Payment and Delivery Pipeline

To set up your payment and delivery pipeline, you'll need to create a unique URL for each product and integrate a payment processor like NOWPayments. You can use a tool like Web3 Wallet Connect ($19.99) to generate a multi-chain QR code that allows buyers to pay in their preferred cryptocurrency. Once you've set up your payment processor, you can automate delivery for your buyers by integrating with a delivery platform like Core Shop Engine – a one-time payment of $49.99 gets you access to this powerful tool.
Traditional payment solutions like PayPal and Stripe are often blocked in West Africa due to geo-restrictions.
Going Live and Making Your First Sale

Once you've set up your payment and delivery pipeline, it's time to go live and start selling your digital products. Create a marketing plan to reach your target audience and promote your products on social media, online forums, and other channels. Make sure to include a clear call-to-action in your marketing materials, directing buyers to your unique URL where they can purchase your products. With a little bit of effort, you'll be making your first sale in no time – and once you have, you can scale your business to reach even more customers.
Scaling Once the System Is Running

Once your payment and delivery pipeline is up and running, you can start scaling your business to reach even more customers. This involves refining your marketing strategy, optimizing your pricing and delivery options, and continually innovating to stay ahead of the competition. By using a tool like Admin Dashboard ($29.99) to track your sales and customer data, you can make data-driven decisions to drive growth and success. With the right tools and mindset, you can take your digital product business to the next level and achieve your dreams.
